data.title: Fed rate-cut odds for May jump as tariffs fuel recession worries
data.description:
Traders ramped up bets that a cut may be a possibility at the Federal Reserve’s upcoming meeting in May, as President Donald Trump announced new sweeping tariffs, igniting fears of a global trade war.

Odds on Thursday of a May rate reduction jumped to 24% from ~11% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool. A week ago, the probability was at ~12%.

Donald Trump detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%, and the highest rate for the latest round of tariffs is 49%.

The tariffs have led to a sell-off in stock index futures, and has also increased worries of a potential recession in the U.S.

For the June meeting, odds are now at 56.5%, lower from a day ago of 60.6%, but odds for a back-to-back May-June quarter-point cut rose to 15%.

Market traders are pricing in three rate cuts for the year, however, odds for four jumped to 30%. 

In March, the Fed officials kept the rate unchanged.

data.title: Lindsay GAAP EPS of $2.44 beats by $0.55, revenue of $187.1M beats by $9.72M
data.description:
• Lindsay press release (NYSE:LNN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $2.44 beats by $0.55.
  • Revenue of $187.1M (+23.5% Y/Y) beats by $9.72M.
  • The backlog of unfilled orders as of February 28, 2025, was $127.0 million compared with $94.2 million as of February 29, 2024. Included in these backlogs are amounts of $11.9 million and $20.3 million, respectively, for orders that are not expected to be fulfilled within the subsequent 12 months. The increase in backlog is primarily attributed to the large irrigation project in the MENA region.

  • Outlook: Mr. Wood concluded, "In February, the USDA released its initial forecast for 2025 U.S. net farm income, which estimated a 29 percent increase compared to 2024. The forecast anticipates higher government support payments, while crop receipts are forecasted to be slightly lower than the prior year. Accordingly, we don't expect this forecast of 2025 U.S. net farm income to result in a meaningful improvement in demand for irrigation equipment in North America in the near term. In Brazil, commodity prices are improving, however the rising interest rate environment continues to be a headwind to investment. Elsewhere in international irrigation, we remain encouraged by the growth in developing markets, highlighted by project activity in the MENA region."

  • "Second quarter performance in our infrastructure business supports full year growth for fiscal 2025. Anticipated increased U.S. infrastructure funding, although partially offset by the impact of inflation on construction costs, remains supportive of opportunities for Road Zipper System lease revenues and sales of road safety products. We also remain optimistic regarding our Road Zipper System project sales pipeline; however, the timing of individual projects remains challenging to predict."

data.title: Sequans Communications regains compliance with NYSE
data.description:
• Sequans Communications (NYSE:SQNS) said it has regained compliance with the New York Stock Exchange's continued listing standards.
  • Last year in April, the NYSE had notified the firm that it was not in compliance with its average global market capitalization rule, its minimum stockholders' equity requirement, and the trading price of its ADSs were also below the requirement.

data.title: Farmmi regains compliance with Nasdaq's rule
data.description:
• Farmmi (NASDAQ:FAMI) said it has received a notice from the Nasdaq stating that it has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement.
  • Press Release

data.title: Ford recalls over 105,000 SUVs over seatbelt issues - report
data.description:
Ford (NYSE:F) issued a recall affecting approximately 105,322 Ford Expedition and Lincoln Navigator SUVs due to potential seatbelt concerns, Reuters reported, citing the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.

The recall impacts above models from years 2018 to 2020.

The issue involves seat belt pretensioners for drivers and/or front passengers, which may inadvertently lock and prevent the seat belt from retracting or extending properly.  This malfunction could compromise the safety of occupants in the event of a crash.

The dealers will check the date codes on the seat belt retractors and repair any damaged parts at no cost, according to the report.

data.title: Cryptocurrency-linked stocks down as Trump unveils new tariffs
data.description:
Cryptocurrency-linked stocks fell in the U.S. premarket trading hours as President Donald Trump unveiled his long-awaited plans for new tariffs on Wednesday. 

Trump detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%, and the highest rate for the latest round of tariffs is 49%.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) swung between gains and losses on Wednesday, finally settling at $82.5K after the announcement. At the time of writing, it was trading at $83.6K.

The cryptocurrency stayed above the $80K mark. 

The stock index futures tumbled on Thursday, as Trump's reciprocal tariffs ignited fears of a potential global trade war.

Crypto-linked stocks, however, were down: MARA (NASDAQ:MARA) -5.9%, Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) -5.4%, Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) -5%, Bitfarms (NASDAQ:BITF) -4.6%, CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) -6.4%, Bitdeer Technologies  (NASDAQ:BTDR) -3.6%, MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) -4.1%, and HIVE Digital  (NASDAQ:HIVE) -6.2%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Chip stocks tumble despite dodging Trump's tariffs
data.description:
Chip stocks slumped premarket on Thursday after President Donald Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs were announced on trading partners, including China and Taiwan, despite semiconductors being listed among imports exempted from the tariffs.

AI chipmakers Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell about 4%, each. In addition, Marvell Technology (MRVL), Broadcom (AVGO) and Qualcomm (QCOM) tumbled about 4%, each.

Trump's tariffs will impact key nations in the semiconductor supply chain, including 34% on China, 32% on Taiwan and 24% on Japan. India was hit with 26% tariffs.

"To any company that objects to our reciprocal tariffs, then you can build your plant here, and we cut the tariffs to zero," Trump said on Wednesday while announcing the tariff plans.

Shares of Micron (MU) slumped around 5% while Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) declined roughly 4%. GlobalFoundries (GFS), Analog Devices (ADI) and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) each fell nearly 3%. Texas Instruments (TXN) slipped about 2% and Intel (INTC) dipped around 1%.

Meanwhile, chip equipment makers: Lam Research (LRCX), Applied Materials (AMAT) and KLA (KLAC) each declined roughly 3%. Dutch company ASML (ASML) fell about 2%, premarket on Thursday.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Acuity Non-GAAP EPS of $3.73 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.01B misses by $20M
data.description:
• Acuity press release (NYSE:AYI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.73 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.01B (+11.5% Y/Y) misses by $20M.
  • ABL generated net sales of $840.6 million in the second quarter of fiscal 2025, a decrease of $2.9 million, or 0.3 percent, compared to the prior year.
  • AIS generated net sales of $171.5 million in the second quarter of fiscal 2025, an increase of $103.4 million, or 151.8 percent, compared to the prior year.
  • Net cash from operating activities was $191.6 million for the first six months of fiscal 2025.

data.title: Cognyte projects 12% FY26 revenue growth with $43M adjusted EBITDA target
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cognyte Software Ltd. (CGNT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Elad Sharon reported double-digit revenue growth for Q4 2025, with revenue increasing by 13% year-over-year to $94.5 million. Adjusted EBITDA grew 114% year-over-year, reaching $9.3 million. The company generated $19 million in cash flow from operations during the quarter.
  • The company secured major deals in Q4, including a $10 million technology upgrade and multi-year support agreement with a law enforcement agency in EMEA, and five additional deals valued at $5 million or more across various regions.
  • For fiscal year 2025, revenue grew 12% year-over-year to $351 million, with adjusted EBITDA tripling to $29 million. Non-GAAP gross profit rose by 15% year-over-year, reaching $249 million.
  • CFO David Abadi highlighted the company's strong cash position of $113.3 million and no debt. Deferred revenue at the end of Q4 stood at $130.3 million, up from $123.1 million the previous year.
  • The company initiated its share repurchase program during Q4, buying back 586,000 ordinary shares for $5.3 million out of the approved $20 million program.

Outlook

  • For fiscal 2026, Cognyte projects revenue of approximately $392 million, plus or minus 2%, representing around 12% year-over-year growth at the midpoint. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to reach $43 million, a 45% increase year-over-year.
  • Management anticipates total software revenue will make up approximately 87% of total revenue, with professional services contributing the remaining 13%.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin for fiscal 2026 is expected to improve to approximately 71.5%, driven by a better revenue mix and cost structure.
  • Q1 2026 revenue is expected to align with Q4 2025 levels, with sequential growth in subsequent quarters.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2025 software revenue increased by $9.6 million year-over-year to $83.3 million, representing 88% of total revenue. Recurring revenue reached $47.3 million, or 50% of Q4 revenue, driven by support contracts and subscription offerings.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin for the quarter was 71.5%, with software gross margin improving to 78.9% year-over-year.
  • Professional services revenue grew by $1.2 million year-over-year to $11.2 million, with its gross margin improving significantly to 16.3%.
  • Total RPO at the end of Q4 was $545.8 million, supported by a $20 million annual value contract renewal for three years.

Q&A

  • Mike Cikos, Needham: Asked about U.S. demand trends and potential impacts of policy uncertainties. CEO Sharon stated that U.S. sales cycles are longer due to penetration efforts but noted positive engagement with federal agencies. Sharon emphasized the company's focus on expanding its U.S. market presence and investments in hiring, POCs, and participation in industry conferences.
  • Peter Levine, Evercore: Inquired about demand drivers in the U.S. and globally. Sharon highlighted strong global demand driven by increasing data complexity and advanced analytics needs. The U.S. market was noted for its emphasis on border control and organized crime challenges.
  • Shaw Yale, TD Cowen: Asked about market dynamics outside the U.S. Sharon described robust global demand, particularly for advanced tools to handle growing data volumes and adversarial sophistication, including the use of AI.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in the U.S. market potential, raising questions about demand stability and sales cycle length. The tone was generally inquisitive but optimistic about the company’s growth prospects.
  • Management displayed confidence in fiscal 2026 projections, emphasizing positive engagement with customers and a steady expansion strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the sentiment remained positive, with management maintaining a consistent focus on execution and growth initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue grew sequentially from Q3 2025 to Q4 2025, with Q4 revenue reaching $94.5 million compared to $89 million in Q3. Adjusted EBITDA also increased significantly quarter-over-quarter.
  • Fiscal 2026 guidance reflects continued double-digit growth expectations, consistent with trends observed in previous quarters.
  • Management tone remained consistent, with an emphasis on expanding U.S. market penetration and leveraging growth opportunities globally.
  • Analysts focused more heavily on U.S. demand trends this quarter, reflecting increased interest in specific regional growth strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged longer sales cycles in the U.S., particularly with federal agencies, due to rigorous testing and validation processes.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential impacts of policy uncertainties and attrition on U.S. market penetration.
  • Management outlined strategies to mitigate these challenges, including investments in local sales teams, partnerships, and marketing efforts.

Final Takeaway

Cognyte Software Ltd. delivered strong Q4 2025 results with robust revenue growth, profitability improvements, and significant customer wins. Looking ahead, the company is projecting 12% revenue growth and a 45% increase in adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2026, supported by its expanding U.S. presence and global demand for advanced analytics solutions. Management remains confident in its strategy to drive sustained growth and operational efficiency.
